findings.description: Four distinct tint/transparency specifications (headline 40% tint, Light Blue callout 70% tint, Golden Sand callout 30% tint, Wave layers 70% transparency) are not represented as their own rules; only the umbrella 'no other ad-hoc tints' constraint (from u_0005) was extracted. These are separate, specific value constraints per element type and should be split into distinct rules rather than collapsed or omitted.

new_rules.value: ~40% tint of headline color (Gold or Brand Blue)

findings.description: The rule 'specified_tints_only' is a governance/compliance statement ('No other ad-hoc tints — use palette values or these specified tints only') but lacks explicit facets tying it to the enumerated tint values it references (u_0001-u_0004) and lacks scope defining what counts as an ad-hoc vs specified tint. Without linking to the specific tint rules, its enforcement scope is ambiguous.

payload.scope: all tint/transparency usage across brand materials

findings.description: Heading 'Tint Rules' establishes the section scope but no rule/cluster references it as a grouping anchor; the single extracted rule does not reflect that this is a section-level constraint set covering multiple element types, leading to loss of the section-level relation context.
